skill.name: azure-mgmt-apicenter-dotnet
skill.description: Guides developers through Azure API Center management with the Azure.ResourceManager.ApiCenter SDK for .NET, including services, workspaces, APIs, versions, definitions, environments, deployments, and metadata schemas.
skill.summary: Build and manage Azure API Center resources with .NET SDK examples.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer flagged Markdown backticks, sample URLs, weak-crypto keywords, and one error logging example. Review found these are documentation examples for Azure API Center SDK usage, not executable skill code, obfuscation, credential exfiltration, prompt injection, or reconnaissance. The skill is safe to publish with low risk because it encourages package installation and Azure resource operations that users should run only in intended subscriptions.

low_findings.title: Markdown Install and Code Examples Trigger Command Rule
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The external command matches are Markdown code fences, inline type names, and dotnet package installation examples. They are not executed by the skill, but users may run the install commands during normal SDK setup.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are documentation blocks and inline Markdown references in SKILL.md. No script file, command dispatcher, dynamic shell interpolation, or automatic execution path is present.

low_findings.title: Example and Reference URLs Trigger Network Rule
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The hardcoded URLs are sample API documentation links, Azure portal references, and official package or source links. They do not send secrets or collected data to an external endpoint.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible documentation values and references. The skill contains no hidden request logic or data collection behavior around these URLs.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto and Reconnaissance Matches Are Textual False Positives
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The high-severity weak-crypto matches occur inside ordinary descriptions, lifecycle labels, or API governance guidance. The reconnaissance match is an error-handling example that prints an Azure exception message.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched lines do not instantiate cryptographic primitives, hash functions, scanners, host inventory commands, or network discovery logic. Confidence is slightly lower because the static labels were broad, but semantic review found no supporting code.

content.value_statement: Teams need consistent guidance when managing API inventory, versions, deployments, and metadata in Azure API Center. This skill provides focused .NET SDK examples for creating and organizing those resources.
actual_capabilities: Shows how to install Azure.ResourceManager.ApiCenter and Azure.Identity packages.
Creates API Center services, workspaces, APIs, and API versions with .NET examples.
Imports and exports API definitions such as OpenAPI specifications.
Models environments, deployments, runtime URLs, and onboarding links.
Defines metadata schemas for API governance fields and required assignments.
Lists APIs, versions, environments, and deployments from an API Center workspace.
limitations: Does not provision Azure subscriptions, permissions, or resource groups for the user.
Does not validate live Azure API Center service quotas or regional availability.
Examples require users to adapt names, IDs, and governance metadata to their tenant.
Does not replace Azure security review for production API inventory changes.

use_cases.title: Create a governed API catalog
use_cases.description: Set up API Center services, workspaces, APIs, versions, and metadata for a platform team.

use_cases.title: Register API deployments
use_cases.description: Connect API definitions to production, staging, or testing environments with runtime and portal links.

use_cases.title: Automate SDK-based inventory tasks
use_cases.description: Use .NET examples to list APIs, import specifications, and keep lifecycle stages current.

prompt_templates.title: Create a basic API Center service
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to create an Azure API Center service with the .NET SDK. Include required packages, authentication, and a minimal service example.

prompt_templates.title: Add an API with version metadata
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me register a REST API in Azure API Center using .NET. Include workspace, API, version, contact, lifecycle stage, and custom metadata fields.

prompt_templates.title: Import an OpenAPI definition
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a .NET workflow for adding an API definition to an Azure API Center version and importing an OpenAPI file from disk.

prompt_templates.title: Design a governance automation flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a .NET automation flow that creates metadata schemas, registers environments, links deployments, and lists APIs by lifecycle stage.

output_examples.input: Create a new API Center service for a platform team.
output_examples.output: A step-by-step .NET SDK plan covering package installation, DefaultAzureCredential setup, resource group lookup, service data creation, and operation completion.

output_examples.input: Register the Orders API and its production deployment.
output_examples.output: A structured workflow describing workspace selection, API creation, version creation, OpenAPI import, environment setup, and deployment linking.

output_examples.input: Add governance metadata requirements for APIs.
output_examples.output: A concise design for metadata fields such as owning team, cost center, and data classification, with guidance on required assignments.

best_practices: Use managed identity and DefaultAzureCredential for Azure authentication examples.
Keep API lifecycle stages, versions, metadata, and deployment links current.
Review generated resource names and subscription IDs before running Azure operations.
anti_patterns: Do not run generated Azure changes against production without tenant review.
Do not store secrets or credentials in API metadata, URLs, or specification files.
Do not treat sample example.com URLs as production endpoint configuration.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps generate .NET SDK guidance for managing Azure API Center resources and API inventory workflows.

faq.question: Does it run Azure commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides documentation and code examples. Users decide what to run in their own environment.

faq.question: Which SDK package does it focus on?
faq.answer: It focuses on Azure.ResourceManager.ApiCenter with Azure.Identity for authentication examples.

faq.question: Can it import OpenAPI specifications?
faq.answer: It shows the .NET workflow for creating an API definition and importing specification content.

faq.question: Is it only for REST APIs?
faq.answer: No. The referenced API kinds include REST, GraphQL, gRPC, SOAP, webhook, WebSocket, and MCP.

faq.question: What should users review before applying examples?
faq.answer: Users should review subscription IDs, resource names, permissions, metadata, URLs, and production change controls.
